content: Hey, I need some help figuring out my budget. I just got back from a three-month sabbatical in Eastern Europe and my finances are a mess.
Welcome back! A sabbatical sounds amazing. What countries did you visit in Eastern Europe?
I spent most of my time in Poland, Czech Republic, and Hungary. I was doing some pro bono legal work for immigrant rights organizations there.
That's incredible work you're doing. How's your current financial situation looking after the trip?
Not great. I'm 28, work as a civil rights attorney in Chicago, and I've got about $15,000 in credit card debt from the trip. Plus my rent just went up to $1,800 a month.
That's a tough spot. Do you have any other income sources or savings to help manage this?
I do have a small side business selling honey from my backyard beehives. Makes about $200 a month. And I've got $5,000 in savings.
Beekeeping as a side hustle is really cool! Have you considered expanding that to help with your debt?
I've thought about it. I'm also doing open mic nights at a comedy club downtown twice a month. That brings in another $150-200.
Wow, you're really diversifying your income! What's your monthly take-home pay from your attorney job?
About $4,200 after taxes. I'm also trying to save for a climbing trip to Patagonia next year, which I estimate will cost around $3,500.
That's a lot to balance. Would you like me to help create a detailed budget plan that accounts for your debt payoff, savings goals, and living expenses?
Yes, please. I really need a structured plan to get back on track.
